name: changelog
description: Draft a CHANGELOG.md entry for the current branch's commits.
prompt_template:
Draft a `CHANGELOG.md` entry covering the commits on the current branch (`git log main..HEAD --oneline`).

Match the existing format: skim the last ~80 lines of `CHANGELOG.md` to pick up the version-prefix convention, the section headings, and the line voice. Don't invent a new shape.

Distil the commits into a coherent narrative, not a verbatim list. Each line should tell a future reader *why* something landed, not just *what*. Group related commits under one bullet when they belong together; split a single commit into multiple bullets when its diff covers genuinely independent changes.

If the branch has no commits ahead of `main`, say so and don't fabricate an entry.
